global.title: Development Approaches

coreIdeas.contextPhrase: The blockchain inheritance — every state change is preserved as an append-only event, providing the transparent and auditable history that decentralized organizations require for governance and accountability
The sync innovation — DocSync runs P2P without a centralized server and is storage-agnostic, so there is no vendor lock-in; users choose decentralized, centralized, or local storage

The key divergence from blockchain — global consensus limits speed and scalability, so Powerhouse prioritizes local-first workflows where contributors operate efficiently without waiting for network agreement
The modeling layer — MDD uses abstract models (similar to GraphQL schemas) as blueprints for workflows and data, enabling early validation and cross-functional alignment before code is written
The acceleration layer — RAD eliminates backend complexity through automated code generation from document models, letting developers focus on UI/UX while APIs and data structures stay in sync
The scaling pattern — CQRS isolates write operations (validated and stored as events) from read models (optimized for analytics, search, or operational views), so each scales independently
The responsiveness pattern — EDA lets processes like validation, notifications, and analytics run in parallel without blocking each other, with fault isolation so failures don't cascade
MCP code submission format: body only, no function wrapper — CLI/MCP injects the harness
Import placement rule: external deps go in src/ reducer files, not in SET_OPERATION_REDUCER calls
Error classes resolve by name reference at runtime — explicit imports cause double-registration
Naming convention: PascalCase + Error suffix required for auto-import resolution to work
Testing gotcha: reducer errors are recorded, not thrown — toThrow always passes, hiding real failures
Operation design principle: one op per user action keeps concerns separated and replay meaningful
Operation naming and documentation standard with examples and error declarations
Determinism rule: crypto.randomUUID and Math.random break replay — all randomness from action input
Subtle variant: fallback with non-deterministic default (e.g. ?? Date.now()) is equally dangerous
TypeScript pattern: truthy checks handle both undefined and null for optional reducer fields
Error classes auto-resolve by name — explicit imports cause double-registration bugs
Testing uses Vitest with generateMock for type-safe test fixtures

global.description: Blockchain-inspired patterns (event sourcing, DocSync), MDD, RAD, CQRS, and EDA as the technical methodology behind Powerhouse.
global.orientation: Powerhouse's development methodology draws from blockchain principles while diverging where global consensus is impractical. Event sourcing provides immutable audit trails. DocSync enables P2P synchronization without server dependency. Local-first workflows prioritize contributor efficiency over global coordination. On top of this, Model-Driven Development uses abstract models as shared blueprints across teams, RAD accelerates iteration through code generation and pre-built frameworks, CQRS separates reads from writes for independent scaling, and EDA decouples components so validation, notifications, and analytics run in parallel. These approaches combine to make Powerhouse systems responsive, auditable, and scalable without the performance bottlenecks of on-chain consensus.
